title: ON THE QUANTITATIVE ESTIMATION OF BERYLLIUM. PART I.  AS BARIUM FLUOBERYLLATE GRAVIMETRICALLY
author: R. K. DUTTTA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A gravimetric method for the estimation of beryllium has been described. Beryllium with fluoride ion affords a BeF 4  2-  ion, and when a barium salt is added to this solution, it effects a quantitative preci­pitation of BaBeF 4 . The  P  H  of the solution was maintained at approx. 4. The formation of barium fluoride and the corroding action of hydrofluoric acid on glass are prevented by addition of boric acid.
steps_raw: A gravimetric method for the estimation of beryllium has been described. Beryllium with fluoride ion affords a BeF 4  2-  ion, and when a barium salt is added to this solution, it effects a quantitative preci­pitation of BaBeF 4 . The  P  H  of the solution was maintained at approx. 4. The formation of barium fluoride and the corroding action of hydrofluoric acid on glass are prevented by addition of boric acid.
